data.title: As a DSO plugin developer, I can check if a ticket exists via the CLI in sub-millisecond time
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add `ticket exists <id>` subcommand that returns exit 0 if the ticket exists (directory + CREATE event file present) and non-zero otherwise. Implementation does NOT invoke ticket-reducer.py — pure filesystem check for sub-millisecond performance.

**Why**: Enables pre-commit-ticket-gate.sh (and other hot-path callers) to replace direct filesystem checks with a stable CLI contract, without paying the reducer cost.

**Scope**:
- IN: ticket-exists.sh (or Python) implementation, dispatcher integration, integration test exercising the subcommand through the CLI dispatcher against a synthetic fixture, unit tests
- OUT: Refactoring pre-commit-ticket-gate.sh itself (that migration is Epic e1b2-2653's concern)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `.claude/scripts/dso ticket exists <id>` returns exit 0 for existing tickets and a non-zero exit code for missing ones
  ← Satisfies: "exists (O(1) ticket existence check via directory + CREATE-file presence without invoking the reducer — replaces direct filesystem checks in pre-commit-ticket-gate.sh)"
- When this story is complete, a single lookup completes in under 10ms on a representative tracker (benchmarked and documented)
  ← Satisfies: "preserving sub-millisecond performance for pre-commit hooks" (from the Approach section)
- When this story is complete, an integration test exercises the subcommand through the CLI dispatcher against a synthetic .tickets-tracker/ fixture, asserting exit-code contract correctness
  ← Satisfies: "Each new subcommand has an integration test in tests/ that exercises the subcommand through the ticket CLI dispatcher against a synthetic .tickets-tracker/ fixture directory"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Performance] Sub-millisecond target requires avoiding ticket-reducer.py invocation — directory + CREATE-file presence check only
- [Testing] Integration test must follow the existing pattern (see test-sprint-next-batch.sh) for synthetic fixture setup and CLI dispatch

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
